name: Eilean Mor Lighthouse
description: The Eilean Mor Lighthouse mystery refers to the unexplained disappearance of three lighthouse keepers from the Flannan Isles Lighthouse in December 1900. The lighthouse, located on a remote island in the Outer Hebrides of Scotland, was found abandoned with the keepers vanished without a trace. Proponents of paranormal explanations suggest theories ranging from sea monsters and alien abduction to ghostly apparitions and other supernatural forces. The mystery has captured imaginations for over a century, inspiring countless fictional accounts and fueling speculation. The mainstream explanation is that the keepers were likely swept away by a rogue wave during a storm while securing equipment near the western landing stage. This theory is supported by evidence of storm damage and the fact that at least one keeper was known to have gone outside against regulations. Further supporting the wave theory is the finding of upturned chairs, an overturned table, and an untouched meal, suggesting a sudden event. However, the lack of bodies and the seemingly inexplicable circumstances surrounding the abandonment continue to fuel alternative theories. The official inquiry attributed the disappearance to accidental drowning, but the lack of definitive answers keeps the mystery alive in popular culture.
subtitle: The Vanishing Lighthouse Keepers of Eilean Mor

claims.claim: The keepers were abducted by aliens.
claims.evidence: No bodies were found, and the circumstances are too bizarre to be explained by natural causes.
claims.criticism: Lack of any credible evidence of alien involvement. The absence of bodies can be attributed to the strong currents and tides around the island.

claims.claim: A sea monster or other mythical creature dragged the keepers into the sea.
claims.evidence: The remote location and the wild seas surrounding the island lend themselves to such speculation.
claims.criticism: No evidence of any such creature exists. This is purely speculative.

claims.claim: The keepers were victims of a supernatural event or curse.
claims.evidence: The Flannan Isles have a history of folklore and superstition.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence to support this claim. Attributing the event to a curse is a simplistic and unsubstantiated explanation.

keyFigures.name: Patrick Moore
keyFigures.role: Captain of the Hesperus, the ship that discovered the lighthouse abandoned.

keyFigures.name: Walter Aldebert
keyFigures.role: Relieving keeper who first reported the disappearance.

keyFigures.name: Brian Dunning
keyFigures.role: Science writer who debunks paranormal claims surrounding the event.

mythologyConnections: Selkies (as a potential explanation for missing persons)

timeline.event: Relief ship 'Hesperus' arrives and discovers the lighthouse abandoned.

sources.author: Richard Wilson
sources.work: The Vanishing Lighthousemen of Eilean Mor
sources.title: Richard Wilson
